question: On the Ubuntu container, configure the eth0 interface with a static IP address 172.16.120.10/24.
hint: You can use nmcli or ip addr add. Remember to bring the interface up if needed.

question: On the Rocky container, configure the eth0 interface with a static IP address 172.16.120.11/24.
hint: Use nmcli or ip commands to set the address and prefix.

question: On the Ubuntu container, set the default gateway to 172.16.120.1 and configure DNS to use 1.1.1.1.
hint: Use ip route add default via 172.16.120.1 and add 'nameserver 1.1.1.1' to /etc/resolv.conf.
validation_script:
#!/bin/bash
GATEWAY=$(ip route show | grep '^default' | awk '{print $3}')
DNSOK=$(grep -q 'nameserver 1.1.1.1' /etc/resolv.conf && echo yes || echo no)
if [[ "$GATEWAY" == "172.16.120.1" && "$DNSOK" == "yes" ]]; then exit 0; else exit 1; fi

question: Verify network connectivity: ping the gateway (172.16.120.1) and an external host (8.8.8.8) from the Ubuntu container, each with two packets.
hint: Run ping -c 2 172.16.120.1 and ping -c 2 8.8.8.8. Both should succeed.
validation_script:
#!/bin/bash
ping -c 2 172.16.120.1 >/dev/null 2>&1
GW=$?
ping -c 2 8.8.8.8 >/dev/null 2>&1
EXT=$?
if [[ $GW -eq 0 && $EXT -eq 0 ]]; then exit 0; else exit 1; fi
